mnemonics.title: ARMstrong AXIOMS
mnemonics.content: R.A.T - Reflexivity (Y⊆X → X→Y), Augmentation (X→Y → XZ→YZ), Transitivity (X→Y, Y→Z → X→Z)

mnemonics.title: FD Rules Made Easy
mnemonics.content: U-D-P: Union (X→Y, X→Z → X→YZ), Decomposition (X→YZ → X→Y, X→Z), Pseudotransitive (X→Y, WY→Z → WX→Z)

mnemonics.title: Finding Closure - Process
mnemonics.content: START with X, REPEAT: scan FDs, add new attributes, STOP when no change - that's X+!

mnemonics.title: Key Detection - AB+ Approach
mnemonics.content: To find keys: Start with attributes, add from FDs until ALL covered - minimal = candidate key

acronyms.usage: X→Y means X functionally determines Y
Minimal superkey that can uniquely identify tuples
Selected candidate key used for identification
Set of attributes that uniquely identifies tuples in a relation
The determining attributes in an FD (X in X→Y)
The determined attributes in an FD (Y in X→Y)

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with X inside. Add attributes from RHS of FDs when LHS is satisfied. Keep looping until no new attributes can be added. Final set is X+.
Create table with R1 attributes as columns, mark 'a' for R1 attrs, 'b' for R2-R1 attrs. Apply FDs to change 'b' to 'a'. If any row becomes all 'a', lossless join is guaranteed.
Step 1: Split FDs to singleton RHS. Step 2: Remove extraneous LHS attrs. Step 3: Remove redundant FDs. Result is minimal cover.
List all attributes, find those that can reach ALL attributes via FDs. Start with attributes not on RHS, try combining with others until closure = all attrs.

analogies.analogy: Like a fingerprint - if you know the fingerprint (X), you can uniquely identify the person (Y). Same X always maps to same Y.
Candidate key is like the smallest key that fits a lock. Superkey is any key (including larger ones) that also works.
Like splitting a photo into two parts - lossless means when we rejoin them, we get the EXACT same picture back, no distortion.
Like following recipes - we split the cooking into stations, but the final dish should have all original flavors (dependencies).
